This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Heptaptera anisopetala include alkaloids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
